conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have two kids - Alex who is 5 years old, and Blake who is 10. They both live with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have Alex (5 years old) and Blake (10 years old) living with you.

Before I can properly assess your eligibility, I need to read the official guidance on Child Benefit rules. Let me do that now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have a few questions to help me assess your eligibility:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. They both live with me and I'm the one looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or care. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year?** 

(This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge, so it's important to be aware of.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into all my financial details right now - I'm mainly just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim it in the first place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - I understand. I have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:

- You have two children: **Alex (5 years old)** and **Blake (10 years old)**
- Both children live with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for them
- Neither child is in hospital or care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's why:
- **Alex (5 years old)**: You meet all the requirements - Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming for them.
- **Blake (10 years old)**: You meet all the requirements - Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming for them.

You can apply for Child Benefit for both children. Only one person can receive Child Benefit per child, and since no one else is claiming for Alex or Blake, you should be able to claim successfully.

**Please note:** While I haven't asked about your income details, if you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive the benefit, but you might need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
